title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Program Brief: Repository Baseline
summary: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository baseline increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Repository Baseline · Theory Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6843 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 6843 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Repository Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_outcomes_repo_accessibility_4(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-repository-baseline-okhbe7
# Competency: asteroids-repository-baseline.
# Case 7269: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_repository_baseline_okhbe7(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_okhbe7 = "okhbe7-7ebhko-7269"
    assert evidence_variant_okhbe7
    evidence_axes_okhbe7 = (
        "playerokhbe7",
        "seed7269",
        "input7ebhko",
        "frameokhbe77269",
        "state7269okhbe7",
        "playtest7ebhko7269",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_okhbe7)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Repository Baseline: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8571 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
